prompt:
You are an expert LLVM compiler engineer, specializing in LLVM IR, InstCombine, and missed-optimization analysis.

I found a **new missed optimization in InstCombine** and currently have **only one LLVM IR example**.

Your tasks:

1) Understand the missed optimization from my single example.
2) Generalize it into a reusable pattern and summarize the pattern so it could be implemented as an InstCombine fold.
3) Generate many additional LLVM IR test cases that are semantically equivalent and should trigger the same transformation, including corner cases.

## Reference: common missed elements and patterns

When generalizing, consider the following summary of elements frequently missed in InstCombine and patterns that are often not well handled. 

The original issue is the concrete idiom:

- `umax(a, 10)` clamps `a` to be at least `10`
- then `add ..., -10` subtracts that same clamp value

So `umax(a, C) - C` is really `max(a - C, 0)`, i.e. unsigned saturating subtract: `llvm.usub.sat(a, C)`. The motivating test shows this for `i8` and `C=10`.

The patch generalizes that exact shape in `foldAddWithConstant()` by matching:

- an `add` with constant RHS `C`
- where the non-constant operand is `m_OneUse(m_UMax(X, -C))`

and rewrites:

- `add (umax X, -C), C` with `C` negative
- into `llvm.usub.sat(X, -C)`

Key points:
- it is not a one-off for `10`; it works for any integer type/constant pair where the add constant is the negation of the `umax` threshold.
- the transform requires exact constant agreement (`umax(X, K) + -K` only).
- `m_OneUse` constrains profitability/safety of replacing the matched `umax`.

The dev tests broaden coverage around that abstraction:
- `fold_add_umax_to_usub`: the direct positive case.
- `fold_add_umax_to_usub_incorrect_rhs`: negative test proving no fold if constants do not match (`10` vs `-11`).
- `fold_add_umax_to_usub_multiuse`: checks the one-use restriction blocks the fold when `umax` has another consumer.
- `uadd_sat_via_add_swapped_cmp_select_nonstrict`: separate generalized saturating-arithmetic coverage showing InstCombine also recognizes equivalent non-intrinsic forms even with swapped operands/non-strict compare structure.

This is a good template: start from one algebraic idiom, encode exact matcher constraints, then add positive/negative/multiuse/equivalent-form tests.

## Output format requirement (strict)

Return **ONLY** JSON (a single JSON object). No Markdown, no extra text.

## What I will provide

- The original LLVM IR snippet (minimal reproducer).
- The target triple/datalayout if relevant. 

## What you must output (in JSON)

Include the following top-level keys (you may add more keys if useful):

- "pattern" (generalized pattern description)
- "testcases" (an array of testcase objects; include only positive cases)

Each testcase object must include at least:

- "name"
- "ir" (the input IR for this case)
- "expected_optimized_ir" (what InstCombine should produce, at least the key simplified instructions; full function preferred)

---

## Here is my single example 

define i8 @test(i8 %a) {
  %1 = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 10)
  %sel = add i8 %1, -10
  ret i8 %sel
}

define i8 @test(i8 %a) {
  %res = call i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8 %a, i8 10)
  ret i8 %res
}

pattern.summary: Fold an add of a negative constant applied to an unsigned-maximum clamp into unsigned saturating subtract.

constraints: T is any integer scalar or integer vector type supported by the involved intrinsics
C is a constant integer splat/value
C is negative modulo the bitwidth, i.e. there exists K such that K = -C
Y matches llvm.umax(X, K) or llvm.umax(K, X)
The umax threshold K must match exactly the negation of the add constant: K == -C

notes: Works for any integer bitwidth and vector-of-integer type where llvm.umax and llvm.usub.sat exist.
The add constant and umax clamp constant must agree exactly; no fold for mismatched constants.
One-use on the matched umax mirrors the intended InstCombine profitability/safety restriction.
Commuted operand order of the add and of the umax should also be considered equivalent shapes.
Important corner cases include K = 0, K = 1, K = unsigned max, and very small integer widths.

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_basic
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_basic(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 10)
  %r = add i8 %m, -10
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_umax_operands_swapped
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_umax_operands_swapped(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 10, i8 %a)
  %r = add i8 %m, -10
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_add_operands_swapped
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_add_operands_swapped(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 10)
  %r = add i8 -10, %m
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i16_basic
testcases.ir:
declare i16 @llvm.umax.i16(i16, i16)
declare i16 @llvm.usub.sat.i16(i16, i16)

define i16 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i16_basic(i16 %a) {
  %m = call i16 @llvm.umax.i16(i16 %a, i16 1234)
  %r = add i16 %m, -1234
  ret i16 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i32_basic
testcases.ir:
declare i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32, i32)
declare i32 @llvm.usub.sat.i32(i32, i32)

define i32 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i32_basic(i32 %a) {
  %m = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 %a, i32 100000)
  %r = add i32 %m, -100000
  ret i32 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i64_basic
testcases.ir:
declare i64 @llvm.umax.i64(i64, i64)
declare i64 @llvm.usub.sat.i64(i64, i64)

define i64 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i64_basic(i64 %a) {
  %m = call i64 @llvm.umax.i64(i64 %a, i64 4294967296)
  %r = add i64 %m, -4294967296
  ret i64 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i3_small_width
testcases.ir:
declare i3 @llvm.umax.i3(i3, i3)
declare i3 @llvm.usub.sat.i3(i3, i3)

define i3 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i3_small_width(i3 %a) {
  %m = call i3 @llvm.umax.i3(i3 %a, i3 5)
  %r = add i3 %m, -5
  ret i3 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i1_zero_threshold
testcases.ir:
declare i1 @llvm.umax.i1(i1, i1)
declare i1 @llvm.usub.sat.i1(i1, i1)

define i1 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i1_zero_threshold(i1 %a) {
  %m = call i1 @llvm.umax.i1(i1 %a, i1 0)
  %r = add i1 %m, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_zero_threshold
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_zero_threshold(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 0)
  %r = add i8 %m, 0
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_threshold_one
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_threshold_one(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 1)
  %r = add i8 %m, -1
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_threshold_allones
testcases.ir:
declare i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8, i8)
declare i8 @llvm.usub.sat.i8(i8, i8)

define i8 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i8_threshold_allones(i8 %a) {
  %m = call i8 @llvm.umax.i8(i8 %a, i8 -1)
  %r = add i8 %m, 1
  ret i8 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_i16_threshold_allones
testcases.ir:
declare i16 @llvm.umax.i16(i16, i16)
declare i16 @llvm.usub.sat.i16(i16, i16)

define i16 @fold_add_umax_to_usub_i16_threshold_allones(i16 %a) {
  %m = call i16 @llvm.umax.i16(i16 -1, i16 %a)
  %r = add i16 1, %m
  ret i16 %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_v4i8_basic
testcases.ir:
declare <4 x i8> @llvm.umax.v4i8(<4 x i8>, <4 x i8>)
declare <4 x i8> @llvm.usub.sat.v4i8(<4 x i8>, <4 x i8>)

define <4 x i8> @fold_add_umax_to_usub_v4i8_basic(<4 x i8> %a) {
  %m = call <4 x i8> @llvm.umax.v4i8(<4 x i8> %a, <4 x i8> <i8 10, i8 10, i8 10, i8 10>)
  %r = add <4 x i8> %m, <i8 -10, i8 -10, i8 -10, i8 -10>
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_v2i16_basic
testcases.ir:
declare <2 x i16> @llvm.umax.v2i16(<2 x i16>, <2 x i16>)
declare <2 x i16> @llvm.usub.sat.v2i16(<2 x i16>, <2 x i16>)

define <2 x i16> @fold_add_umax_to_usub_v2i16_basic(<2 x i16> %a) {
  %m = call <2 x i16> @llvm.umax.v2i16(<2 x i16> <i16 300, i16 300>, <2 x i16> %a)
  %r = add <2 x i16> <i16 -300, i16 -300>, %m
  ret <2 x i16> %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_v8i8_threshold_one
testcases.ir:
declare <8 x i8> @llvm.umax.v8i8(<8 x i8>, <8 x i8>)
declare <8 x i8> @llvm.usub.sat.v8i8(<8 x i8>, <8 x i8>)

define <8 x i8> @fold_add_umax_to_usub_v8i8_threshold_one(<8 x i8> %a) {
  %m = call <8 x i8> @llvm.umax.v8i8(<8 x i8> %a, <8 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1>)
  %r = add <8 x i8> %m, <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>
  ret <8 x i8> %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_v4i32_zero_threshold
testcases.ir:
declare <4 x i32> @llvm.umax.v4i32(<4 x i32>, <4 x i32>)
declare <4 x i32> @llvm.usub.sat.v4i32(<4 x i32>, <4 x i32>)

define <4 x i32> @fold_add_umax_to_usub_v4i32_zero_threshold(<4 x i32> %a) {
  %m = call <4 x i32> @llvm.umax.v4i32(<4 x i32> %a, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer)
  %r = add <4 x i32> %m, zeroinitializer
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}

testcases.name: fold_add_umax_to_usub_v2i64_large_constant
fold_add_umax_to_usub_v16i8_allones_threshold
testcases.ir:
declare <16 x i8> @llvm.umax.v16i8(<16 x i8>, <16 x i8>)
declare <16 x i8> @llvm.usub.sat.v16i8(<16 x i8>, <16 x i8>)

define <16 x i8> @fold_add_umax_to_usub_v16i8_allones_threshold(<16 x i8> %a) {
  %m = call <16 x i8> @llvm.umax.v16i8(<16 x i8> <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>, <16 x i8> %a)
  %r = add <16 x i8> <i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1, i8 1>, %m
  ret <16 x i8> %r
}
